title: Plectronia opima
intro: Plectronia opima, also known as the West Indian jasmine, is a flowering plant species native to tropical regions of the Americas. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Plectronia opima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Plectronia opima is commonly prepared as a tea or a tincture, and is also used in the production of infusions and decoctions to alleviate various health conditions.
taxonomy: Plectronia opima is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the madder family and comprises a large number of flowering plant species. Plectronia opima is part of the genus Plectronia, which is native to tropical regions of Asia. The genus Plectronia belongs to the tribe Psychotrieae, which is a subgroup of the Rubiaceae family. The classification of Plectronia opima is further supported by its characteristic morphology and molecular phylogenetic studies.
